questions.q: The Indian film genre known as the 'masala film' takes its name from what?
options: A mix of spices used in Indian cooking
questions.explanation: The masala film is named after the Indian mixture of spices, reflecting its blend of multiple genres.

questions.q: Which combination of elements best characterises the Indian masala film?
options: Action, romance, comedy, melodrama and song-and-dance
questions.explanation: The masala film blends action, romance, comedy, melodrama and song-and-dance into a single feature.

questions.q: In which decade did the masala film become firmly established as the dominant commercial template of Hindi cinema?
questions.explanation: The masala film was perfected and became dominant in 1970s Hindi cinema.

questions.q: Which director is most associated with perfecting the masala film formula in 1970s Hindi cinema?
questions.explanation: Manmohan Desai is widely regarded as a master of the 1970s-80s masala film.

questions.q: The famous screenwriting duo Salim-Javed, central to the masala film era, was made up of Javed Akhtar and which other writer?
questions.explanation: Salim-Javed comprised Salim Khan and Javed Akhtar, the era's most influential writing team.

questions.q: Which actor became the leading on-screen star of the masala film era as the 'angry young man'?
questions.explanation: Amitabh Bachchan, as the 'angry young man', became the defining star of 1970s masala cinema.

questions.q: The 1977 Manmohan Desai film about three brothers separated in childhood and raised in different faiths, a classic masala film, is titled what?
questions.explanation: Amar Akbar Anthony (1977) is a definitive Manmohan Desai masala film about three separated brothers.

questions.q: Song-and-dance numbers are a core ingredient of the masala film. In Indian cinema, the songs heard in these sequences are typically performed by which kind of artist rather than sung live by the actors?
options: A live orchestra appearing on screen
Playback singers whose voices the actors lip-sync to
questions.explanation: Masala-film song sequences use the playback system: professional playback singers pre-record the songs and on-screen actors lip-sync to them.

questions.q: The masala film template spread well beyond Hindi cinema. In which other Indian film industries did it also become a dominant commercial form?
options: Tamil, Telugu and other Indian-language industries
questions.explanation: The masala template became dominant across Tamil, Telugu and other Indian-language film industries.

questions.q: Roughly how long is a typical masala film, often including an interval?
questions.explanation: Masala films are typically long, often running two-and-a-half to three hours with an interval.

questions.q: The 1975 Salim-Javed-scripted film often called the archetypal masala blockbuster, directed by Ramesh Sippy, is which classic?
questions.explanation: Sholay (1975), written by Salim-Javed and directed by Ramesh Sippy, is a landmark masala blockbuster.

questions.q: The 1973 film Zanjeer, which launched Amitabh Bachchan's 'angry young man' image, was scripted by which writing duo?
questions.explanation: Zanjeer (1973) was written by Salim-Javed and directed by Prakash Mehra.

questions.q: Which 1975 Yash Chopra film, written by Salim-Javed, features the famous masala-era line 'Mere paas maa hai'?
questions.explanation: Deewaar (1975), directed by Yash Chopra and written by Salim-Javed, contains the iconic line 'Mere paas maa hai'.

questions.q: The 'lost and found' plot device, in which family members are separated and reunited, is a recurring feature of which Indian film genre?
questions.explanation: The 'lost and found' separated-family device is a signature trope of the masala film.

questions.q: Manmohan Desai frequently cast which actor as the lead across many of his blockbuster masala films of the 1970s and 80s?
questions.explanation: Manmohan Desai repeatedly cast Amitabh Bachchan in hits such as Amar Akbar Anthony, Naseeb and Coolie.

questions.q: The masala film is generally contrasted with which alternative movement in Indian cinema of the same era?
questions.explanation: Commercial masala films are typically contrasted with the realist parallel cinema or Indian New Wave.

questions.q: Which actor's South Indian films, full of fights, comedy, romance and dance, made him an enduring icon of Tamil masala cinema?
questions.explanation: Rajinikanth became an enduring icon of Tamil masala cinema through his action-comedy entertainers.

questions.q: Which composer duo was heavily associated with scoring the hit songs of many 1970s Hindi masala films?
questions.explanation: Laxmikant-Pyarelal, alongside R.D. Burman, composed the music for many 1970s masala hits.

questions.q: The masala film's structure typically builds toward what kind of resolution?
options: A clear triumph of good over evil and emotional reunion
A tragic death of all main characters
questions.explanation: Masala films usually resolve with good triumphing over evil and emotionally satisfying reunions.

questions.q: Which 1971 film starring Rajesh Khanna and Amitabh Bachchan, blending melodrama, friendship and music, is often cited among the emotional templates feeding into masala storytelling?
questions.explanation: Anand (1971), starring Rajesh Khanna and Amitabh Bachchan, exemplified the emotional melodrama feeding into masala storytelling.

questions.q: Which term is commonly used in Indian cinema for a film that is a major commercial success across the country, the goal of most masala films?
questions.explanation: A successful masala film aims to be a blockbuster or 'all-India hit' across regions and languages.

questions.q: Director Manmohan Desai openly stated that his masala films were chiefly made to appeal to which audience?
options: The common, mass audience seeking pure entertainment
questions.explanation: Desai openly made his masala films for the mass audience seeking entertainment and escapism.

questions.q: The 1983 masala film Coolie, starring Amitabh Bachchan and directed by Manmohan Desai, became notable partly because of what real-life event during its shooting?
questions.explanation: During Coolie's filming, Amitabh Bachchan suffered a near-fatal injury that drew enormous public attention.

questions.q: Before becoming a celebrated lyricist and screenwriter independently, Javed Akhtar's masala-era partnership with Salim Khan broke up in approximately which year?
questions.explanation: The Salim-Javed partnership dissolved around 1982 after a decade of landmark masala scripts.

questions.q: Which 1967 Vijay Anand thriller-musical, blending suspense, romance and song, is cited among the slick templates that fed into the multi-genre masala style?
questions.explanation: Vijay Anand's Jewel Thief (1967) blended suspense, romance and song in a style that fed into the masala approach.

questions.q: In Tamil cinema, which composer's work became inseparable from masala entertainers from the late 1970s onward, scoring hundreds of such films?
questions.explanation: Ilaiyaraaja scored a vast number of Tamil masala entertainers from the late 1970s onward.

questions.q: Which 1979 Manmohan Desai masala film stars Amitabh Bachchan and Shashi Kapoor as brothers separated in childhood and later reunited?
questions.explanation: Suhaag (1979), directed by Manmohan Desai, features Amitabh Bachchan and Shashi Kapoor as separated, reunited brothers.

questions.q: The masala film's appeal to a broad, multilingual audience is often explained by its reliance on which storytelling quality?
options: Universal emotions and spectacle that transcend language
questions.explanation: Masala films rely on broadly legible emotions, action and spectacle that work across language barriers.

questions.q: Filmmaker S.S. Rajamouli's 2010s blockbusters like Baahubali are often described as a modern revival of which Indian film tradition?
questions.explanation: Rajamouli's epics are widely viewed as a contemporary, large-scale revival of the masala spectacle tradition.

questions.q: Why is the masala film considered a distinctly Indian contribution to world cinema rather than a borrowed Hollywood form?
options: It deliberately fuses many genres and song-and-dance into one continuous feature
It is always under one hour long
questions.explanation: Its deliberate all-in-one fusion of multiple genres with integrated song-and-dance marks the masala film as a distinctly Indian form.
